input.raw_input: We need a HIPAA-compliant patient scheduling portal for a regional clinic network. Patients view lab results, book/reschedule appointments, message providers, and request prescription refills. Clinic staff manage appointment availability and review messages. About 25 screens across 4 user roles. Integrations: Epic EHR (FHIR), Stripe billing, Twilio SMS reminders, and Okta SSO. Responsive web only, no mobile app initially.

expected_output: Normalized Stage 2 context: industry=healthcare, project_type=greenfield, ~25 screens, 4 integrations (Epic/FHIR, Stripe, Twilio, Okta), regulatory includes HIPAA. The summary should faithfully capture the portal's purpose, the patient-facing features, the staff admin view, and the integration set without inventing facts (e.g. no mobile app, no claims not in the source).
notes: Prefill — summarization scores the summary against raw_input; extraction_accuracy scores the normalized fields against gold.

input.raw_input: Build a fintech KYC onboarding app for a digital bank. New customers complete identity verification, document upload, sanctions and PEP screening, and risk scoring before an account is opened. Roughly 18 screens across 3 roles: applicant, compliance reviewer, and ops admin. Integrations: Persona for identity verification, Plaid for bank linking, a third-party sanctions-screening API, and an internal core-banking API. SOC 2 and PCI-DSS compliance are required.

expected_output: Normalized Stage 2 context: industry=fintech, project_type=greenfield, ~18 screens, 4 integrations (Persona, Plaid, sanctions API, core-banking), regulatory includes SOC 2 and PCI-DSS. The summary should capture KYC onboarding, IDV, sanctions/PEP screening, risk scoring, the three roles, and the integrations, with no fabricated facts.

input.raw_input: HIPAA-compliant patient scheduling portal for a regional clinic network. 25 screens, 4 roles, integrations with Epic FHIR, Stripe, Twilio, Okta. Responsive web. 20-week target.

expected_output: A coherent staffing plan + roster for a regulated healthcare web build: product, engineering (a mix of senior and mid/junior), UI/UX, QA, and DevOps coverage; a short delivery plan covering discovery, design, build, integrations, compliance, and launch; percentages that sum sensibly and a rationale tied to the project's integration + compliance load.

input.raw_input: Fintech KYC onboarding app for a digital bank. 18 screens, 3 roles, integrations with Persona, Plaid, a sanctions API, and core-banking. SOC 2 + PCI-DSS. 16-week target.

expected_output: A staffing plan + roster suited to a compliance-heavy fintech build: product, engineering, UI/UX, QA, and DevOps/security coverage; a delivery plan addressing IDV, screening, risk scoring, integrations, and SOC 2 / PCI-DSS controls; a rationale that justifies the role mix against the regulatory + integration complexity.

input.raw_input: HIPAA-compliant patient scheduling portal. 25 screens, integrations with Epic FHIR, Stripe, Twilio, Okta. The work needs a dedicated HIPAA compliance lead to own audit logging, BAAs, and PHI handling sign-off.

expected_output: A roster for the regulated portal that DELIBERATELY reuses the org's predefined 'HIPAA Compliance Lead' role — the description explicitly calls for it — by selecting its catalog_role_id, plus engineering/product/UI-UX coverage. The compliance role must be priced at the org's set rate via the explicit selection, not a generic QA grid rate.
notes: Roster agent — gates the catalog-selection behavior (#1 / #2b). roster_catalog_selection asserts the agent set some role's catalog_role_id to 'hipaa_compliance_lead'. SKIPS when no API key (no LLM ran).

input.description: We use Claude Code as an agentic coding assistant for development, CodeRabbit for automated code review, GitHub Copilot autocomplete in the IDE, and Figma AI for design exploration. QA is manual.

expected_output: Per-phase classification: development=agentic (Claude Code), code_review reflects CodeRabbit (chat or agentic), ux_design reflects Figma AI (chat/autocomplete), with autocomplete acknowledged for IDE Copilot. Because this team is clearly AI-forward (agentic tooling), the unmentioned / 'manual' phases (discovery, deployment, qa_testing) are floored to a chat-level baseline rather than left at none. Unknown tools should be empty or minimal, and the notes should justify each mapping.
notes: Tooling classifier — description-only path for an AGENTIC (AI-forward) team. Gold: Claude Code=agentic dev, CodeRabbit=agentic review, Figma AI=chat ux_design; the remaining phases are floored to a chat baseline by _apply_baseline_floor (agentic adoption ⇒ at least chat assist everywhere). classification_accuracy is exact per-phase match.

input.description: The team mostly works manually but occasionally uses ChatGPT and Claude chat to draft and refactor code snippets while writing the application. No other phase uses AI tooling.

expected_output: A conservative classification: development around chat level (occasional LLM chat assistance), other phases none. The reasoning should avoid over-crediting AI tooling given the 'mostly manual' framing.
notes: Tooling classifier — sparse/conservative case. Gold: only development=chat (occasional ChatGPT/Claude chat), all else none.

gap.question_text: How many third-party integrations are in scope and which systems?
Which external systems must we integrate with for this release?
What identity provider and SSO mechanism should we support?
What audit-logging and compliance evidence does HIPAA require here?

expected_output: The two integration questions (integration_scope + integrations) should be clustered/merged into one question since a single answer resolves both; the auth and compliance_audit questions should remain distinct. The merged set should be a valid partition of the inputs with no dropped or duplicated questions.
notes: Consolidator — overlapping integration questions. Gold partition: inputs 0+1 (the two integration questions) merge; 2 (auth) and 3 (compliance_audit) stay distinct → 3 clusters.

gap.question_text: Which identity-verification provider should onboarding use?
How often must sanctions/PEP lists be re-screened against existing customers?
What rules drive the customer risk score and its thresholds?

expected_output: These three questions cover distinct concerns (IDV provider, sanctions re-screening cadence, risk-scoring rules) and should NOT be over-merged; each should remain its own cluster, forming a valid partition of the inputs.
notes: Consolidator — genuinely distinct questions; tests against over-merging. Gold partition: 3 singleton clusters.

input.raw_input: Build + test engagement only: add a bulk CSV import + validation feature to an existing inventory app and an automated test suite for it. Designs and infra already exist; no UX, deployment, or separate code-review phase in scope. ~6 screens touched, brownfield, no new integrations.

expected_output: A lean roster scoped to a build + test engagement: product + engineering anchor the team and a QA role covers the test suite, but there is NO dedicated UI/UX role (ux_design is out of scope) and NO DevOps role (deployment is out of scope). A short plan covering the build and the automated tests, percentages that sum sensibly, and a rationale tied to the dev + QA scope.
notes: Roster agent — phase scoping. With selected_phases=[development, qa_testing], staffing_adequacy relaxes the UI/UX requirement (ux_design out of scope); the judges assess that the team is scoped to build + test (no UX/DevOps-only roles).
